title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
authors: Sneha Samal, Shrikant Joshi, Stefan Björklund, Mohit Chandra, Akhil Bhardwaj, Jaromír Kopeček
abstract: Depositing various coatings on surface of engineering components with the aim to improve their performance concerning wear, corrosion, friction and thermal protection is already a standard practice. Depositing metallic NiTi shape memory alloy coatings may be a viable alternative for hard ceramic coatings. NiTi coatings offer additional benefits originating from unique functional thermomechanical properties. However, fabrication of thick NiTi coatings turned out to be difficult. Standard electroplating and laser cladding methods are not suitable for NiTi the most widely used plasma spray methods tend to produce chemically inhomogeneous coatings that do not transform martensitically, cold sprayed NiTi coatings suffer from poor adhesion to the substrates. In this work we report on first ever successful fabrication of thick NiTi coatings (100-300 um) that display functional thermomechanical properties and simultaneously show very good adherence to the substrate. We used high velocity air fuel thermal spray method to fabricate NiTi coatings deposited on mild steel using four different sets of processing parameters. Chemical composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation and functional thermomechanical properties of the NiTi coatings were evaluated. Although the coatings contain inhomogeneous microstructure, voids, oxide particles, high density of dislocation defects and internal stress, they undergo martensitic transformation upon cooling and or mechanical loading. As sprayed NiTi coatings need to be annealed to display functional thermomechanical properties. Despite their limited tensile strength, the coatings displayed thermal actuation in 3 point bending tests and shape memory effects in nanoindentation and scratch tests.

infographic.key_insight: High-velocity air fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape memory functionality, unlike many prior coating routes.
infographic.engineering_relevance: This enables wear- and corrosion-resistant NiTi surface layers for engineering components while preserving thermal actuation and shape-memory behavior.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel in 4 processing-parameter sets
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Assessed functionality using 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests
infographic.evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated as 100-300 um thick layers and showed martensitic transformation on cooling/mechanical loading, plus thermal actuation and shape memory in mechanical tests.
infographic.watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and require annealing; tensile strength remains limited.
infographic.paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior

zh-Hans.title: HVAF喷涂NiTi涂层：微观结构、相变与形状记忆行为
zh-Hans.abstract: 在工程构件表面沉积各种涂层以改善其耐磨、耐腐蚀、减摩和热防护性能，已是一项标准做法。沉积金属NiTi形状记忆合金涂层可能是硬质陶瓷涂层的可行替代方案。NiTi涂层还具有源于独特功能热机械特性的附加优势。然而，制备厚的NiTi涂层一直很困难。标准电镀和激光熔覆方法并不适用于NiTi；最广泛使用的等离子喷涂方法往往会产生化学成分不均匀、且不能发生马氏体转变的涂层，而冷喷NiTi涂层则存在与基体结合不良的问题。本文报道了首次成功制备出厚NiTi涂层（100-300 um），其不仅表现出功能热机械特性，同时与基体具有很好的附着性。我们采用高速空气燃料热喷涂方法，在低碳钢上以四组不同工艺参数制备NiTi涂层。对NiTi涂层的化学成分、孔隙率、微观结构、相变以及功能热机械特性进行了评估。尽管涂层包含不均匀微观结构、孔隙、氧化物颗粒、高密度位错缺陷和内应力，但在冷却和/或机械加载时仍会发生马氏体转变。喷涂态NiTi涂层需要退火后才能表现出功能热机械特性。尽管其拉伸强度有限，这些涂层在三点弯曲测试中表现出热致驱动，在纳米压痕和划痕测试中表现出形状记忆效应。

infographic.key_insight: 高速空气燃料（HVAF）喷涂能够制备厚的NiTi涂层，并保留马氏体转变和形状记忆功能，不同于许多先前的涂层制备路线。
infographic.engineering_relevance: 这使得工程构件能够获得耐磨、耐腐蚀的NiTi表层，同时保留热致驱动和形状记忆行为。
method: 采用HVAF将NiTi喷涂到低碳钢上，并设置4组工艺参数
表征成分、孔隙率、微观结构、相变和热机械响应
通过三点弯曲、纳米压痕和划痕测试评估其功能性
infographic.evidence_signal: 该涂层被评估为100-300 um厚的层，并在冷却/机械加载时表现出马氏体转变，以及在机械测试中的热致驱动和形状记忆。
infographic.watch_item: 喷涂态涂层的微观结构不均匀，且需要退火；其拉伸强度仍然有限。
